name: Grand Knotmother Aelith
voicePatterns: formal with 'we' — drops the royal 'we' when being honest
small-framed but commands rooms through presence, not volume
careful political language that can shift to blunt honesty without warning
hates being forced into decisions but respects those who force her with good evidence
dialogueHooks: weighing whether to support radical action or preserve institutional stability
demanding evidence before committing guild resources
revealing her five-year correspondence with Thinwalker
making the final political choice based on player evidence

always: continental-scale ward maintenance operations and their status
guild politics across all chapters — who's loyal, who's wavering
the full scope of the Thinning's impact across the region
twenty-two years of institutional compromise and its costs
sometimes: which guild chapters are concealing ward failures
the actual state of the ward network vs what's publicly reported
reform proposals she's shelved for political reasons
rarely: she's had a five-year quiet correspondence with Thinwalker about ward-pattern anomalies
the greatest traditionalist has been secretly collaborating with the greatest heretic
she shares data because the data demands it, regardless of ideology

morning.activity: reviewing chapter reports from across the region

night.activity: reading Thinwalker's latest letter, weighing impossible choices

backstory: Aelith has held the fractious Wardweaver Guild together for twenty-two years through compromise, careful inaction, and an unwillingness to let the perfect be the enemy of the functional. She's small-framed with white coiled hair secured by golden thread-pin wards. Her blackthorn staff has a living ward-thread wrapped around it — one of the few true ward artifacts left. She's a pragmatist, not an ideologue, and when the evidence is compelling enough, she'll back whatever saves the most lives.
secret: Five years ago she began corresponding secretly with Thinwalker — the Frayborn founder. The greatest traditionalist in the Wardweaver world sharing data with the greatest heretic. She does it because the data demands it. Their combined anomaly records are the most complete picture of the Thinning in existence.
motivation: Make the choice that saves the most lives, even if it destroys the institution she built
tensions: 22 years of careful compromise reaching their limit
institutional loyalty vs the evidence that the institution can't solve this alone
secretly collaborating with the faction her guild officially opposes

knownFacts: Sable's institutional loyalty blinds her to the bigger picture
knotmother_sable.history: Aelith appointed Sable because she's competent. She worries Sable's rigidity will become a problem.

knownFacts: Thinwalker's Fray data fills gaps in guild records
Their correspondence would be a scandal if discovered
thinwalker.history: Five years of letters. They've never met in person. Aelith respects Thinwalker's mind and fears what admitting that publicly would cost.
